hook: No will means the state decides what happens to everything.
body: Your home, your savings, your family heirlooms — without a will, none of that goes where you'd choose. A court steps in, and the process can drag on for months while your family waits. Knowing where your plan stands is the first step to fixing it.

hook: What happens to your parents' home if they pass without a plan?
body: It's not a comfortable question, but it's one worth asking before you're in the middle of a crisis. Families without a clear estate plan often face months of legal delays, unexpected costs, and disagreements that could have been avoided. A little preparation now saves a lot of pain later.

hook: A judge you've never met could decide who raises your kids.
body: If you don't name a guardian in your will, that choice goes to a court — not your family. It's one of the most important decisions a parent can make, and it takes less time to set up than you'd think. Don't leave it to chance.

hook: Probate can freeze your family's access to your assets for years.
body: It's a public, court-supervised process that costs time and money — and it's largely avoidable with the right plan in place. Many people don't realize their estate would go through probate until it's too late to change anything. Find out where you stand now.

hook: Being someone's child doesn't give you legal authority over their finances.
body: Without a power of attorney, you may not be able to pay your parent's bills, access their accounts, or make legal decisions — even in an emergency. It's one of the most critical documents in any estate plan, and one of the most commonly missing. Find out what's in place before a crisis forces the question.

hook: Your work life insurance probably isn't enough to protect your family.
body: Employer group coverage is a start, but it rarely replaces what your family would actually need — and it disappears if you change jobs. Life insurance works best when it's part of a real plan, not just a checkbox. Know where your gaps are before you need to find out the hard way.

hook: Trusts aren't just for the wealthy — they're for anyone who wants control.
body: A trust lets you decide exactly how your assets are handled after you're gone, without putting your family through probate court. With taxes on retirement accounts and Social Security in the news, how your estate is structured has real financial consequences. Find out if a trust belongs in your plan.
